message: fix: make primer disclaimer explicit up front (m5wh)

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","title":"IntakePhasesViz: 9-phase flow with drill-in","description":"Implement `IntakePhasesViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Horizontal flow on desktop; vertical stack on mobile.
- 9 nodes: Orientation → People → Assets & Liabilities → Beneficiary Audit → Family Dynamics → Goals & Values → Incapacity → Jurisdiction → Wealth-Tier Routing.
- Each node shows:
  - Phase number + name
  - Approximate time (5 / 10 / 15 min per SKILL.md)
  - An icon from lucide-react
- Connected by arrows. Active phase highlighted.

## Scroll-linked highlighting
The articles sample-session section walks through a narrative that enters each phase in order. Use IntersectionObserver on paragraph markers to highlight the current phase as the reader scrolls.

## Click-to-drill
Click any node → inline panel under the viz shows:
- The agents opening question for that phase (plain English)
- The typical artifact written in that phase (e.g., `intake-record.md` updated, `red-flag-triage.md` created)
- Branch points (e.g., "if user says blended family, trigger overlay")

## Interactions
- Keyboard: arrow keys to walk, Enter to drill.
- Touch: tap to drill; the scroll-link still highlights the phase the reader is "in."
- Reduced motion: no arrow animations; still highlights correctly.

## Acceptance criteria
- Scroll-link works smoothly and doesnt flicker.
- Drill-in copy is accurate to SKILL.md section "The 9 Intake Phases."
- Responsive at 320px through 1440px.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.343393514Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:33:56.340569417Z","close_reason":"Code complete, verified by IvoryOrchid","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:41.162156518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.491176707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.23","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Scroll-linking implementation detail — use IntersectionObserver on [data-intake-phase='N'] markers placed by the sample-session section (hgjp.14). Fall back to click-driven active state if no markers are visible (e.g., if reader jumps from TOC to #produces, which has no intake-phase markers).","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","title":"DeliverablesTreeViz: 45-leaf interactive folder tree","description":"Implement `DeliverablesTreeViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Collapsible folder tree: top-level `my-estate-plan/` → three child folders `intake/`, `analyses/`, `deliverables/`.
- Leaf count: 2 + 20 + 23 = 45 files (exact match to the output contract in SKILL.md).
- Files represented as subtle pill rows with filename, icon, and tier-color (intake = gray, analyses = blue, deliverables = emerald).
- Mode-dependent files show a small "mode:" tag on hover (e.g., `business-continuity-activation.md` shows `mode: business-owner-succession`).

## Filters (toolbar above tree)
- **Show only**: All / Intake / Analyses / Deliverables.
- **Mode filter**: All / new-plan / existing-plan-audit / life-event-delta / urgent-bedside-signing / executor-activation / business-owner-succession / uhnw-restructure / maintenance-review.
- **Search input**: matches filename or plain-English purpose.

## Click-a-leaf behavior
Opens an inline detail card below the tree with:
- Filename
- 2-sentence plain-English purpose
- Representative snippet (2-5 lines) adapted from the template in `assets/` (lightly paraphrased for the web; we do not publish the full templates here — those are behind the paywall).
- Icon link to the relevant section of SKILL.md on the jeffreys-skills.md catalog page.

## Data source
A static const array at the top of the viz file, hand-curated from the bead Im reading now + `assets/` directory listing. Each entry: `{ path, category, purpose, modes[], sampleLines }`. Commit to writing all 45 entries by hand to keep them high-quality; do not scrape.

## Interactions
- Keyboard: tree is a proper `role="tree"` with up/down/left/right.
- Touch: tap to expand/collapse folders; tap leaves to open detail.
- Reduced motion: no expand/collapse animation, snap.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 45 leaves render.
- Filter combinations work (e.g., Mode=executor-activation shows fewer leaves highlighted).
- Detail card accurate.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:42:54.651606478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:42:54.648738348Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:41:57.594122273Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.24","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.626927541Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz: 13-card flip grid","description":"Implement `AntiPatternCardsViz` in `components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.

## Visual design
- Responsive grid (4-col desktop / 2-col tablet / 1-col mobile) of flip-cards.
- ≥13 cards (one per anti-pattern listed in the anti-patterns section bead).
- Front of card: evocative pattern name + one-line hook + emoji/icon.
- Back of card: what actually goes wrong, worst-case outcome, which subagent the skill uses to catch it.

## Interactions
- Hover (desktop) or tap (mobile) to flip. Reduced motion: dissolve instead of 3D flip.
- Keyboard: Tab to focus, Space/Enter to flip.
- Cards are buttons with `aria-pressed` state for screen readers.
- On focus, a subtle ring appears matching the design system accent.

## Data source
Hand-authored array of `{ name, hook, explanation, worstCase, subagent }` objects. Use the 13-card starter list in the anti-patterns section bead.

## Style
Visual weight matches the slack articles callout cards. Each card has a subtle gradient border; hover glow.

## Acceptance criteria
- All cards flip.
- Reading the backs of the 13 cards gives the reader a comprehensive enough picture of the skills risk-scanning layer.
- Accessible: cards should be reachable and readable with keyboard alone.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:19.224994800Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:43:19.214150858Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:08.414151481Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:15.768018958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","title":"OpenGraph + Twitter images (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx, twitter-image.tsx)","description":"Create the two image-route files for the article, modeled on the slack articles equivalents.

## Files
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/opengraph-image.tsx`
- `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/twitter-image.tsx`

Use Nexts `ImageResponse` (next/og) to generate 1200×630 (OG) and 1200×600 (Twitter summary-large-image) cards at build time.

## Design
Match the visual identity of the slack articles OG card but rebrand for wills:
- Dark background (same `#020204` base).
- Title: "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday."
- Subtitle: "AI agents · paid skill · attorney signs the docs."
- Faint illustration echo (could reuse the parrot motif from the hero, or a cleaner document stack).
- Author badge at bottom-left: "Jeffrey Emanuel · jeffreyemanuel.com".

## Dependencies
- `@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp` (from hero-illustration bead).
- Fonts loaded via the `fetch` pattern used in the existing OG files.

## Acceptance criteria
- Both images generated at build time (`bun run build` produces them).
- URLs render correctly when shared on Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Slack, and iMessage.
- File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.052500801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.051860361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

- File sizes kept reasonable (<300 KB each).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:54:02.892028700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:23.857413598Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.26","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.051860361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","title":"CSS styles: decide on sm- reuse vs wep- namespace (globals.css)","description":"Audit `app/globals.css` for every `sm-*` class the slack article uses (sm-scope, sm-body, sm-progress-bar, sm-drop-cap, sm-section-title, sm-subheading, sm-subheading-eyebrow, sm-code-block, sm-table-wrap, sm-table, sm-toc, sm-viz-wrap, sm-callout, sm-callout-emerald, sm-insight-card, sm-sign-off, sm-decisions, sm-display-title, sm-fade-section, sm-visible, sm-reveal-ready, etc.).

## Decision
Two options:
- **Reuse `sm-*`**: simplest. Zero new CSS. Works out of the box if the wills article uses the same visual language.
- **Namespace to `wep-*`**: safer if we expect the wills articles visual identity to diverge (e.g., warmer palette, softer shadows). Adds ~800 lines of CSS.

Default: reuse. Only introduce `wep-*` if a section bead requires a visually distinct style that cannot be expressed as a modifier class (e.g., `sm-insight-card sm-insight-card--wep-warm`).

## Action
- Read `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`: list every `sm-` class referenced.
- Run the article through a dry build; confirm no missing classes.
- If any warm-palette variants are needed (e.g., an amber-accented callout for the "if I die tomorrow" deliverable), add them as modifier classes on the existing `sm-` definitions.

## Acceptance criteria
- Article renders correctly with the existing stylesheet.
- No CSS duplication unless strictly required.
- Any new additions documented with a brief comment at the top of the new block.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.190879525Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:41:14.186701102Z","close_reason":"Audited the wills article against the shared sm-* stylesheet surface. The article's current classes are already covered by app/globals.css, so the right decision is to reuse the existing sm-* namespace with no new wep-* block or duplicate CSS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:38.052564575Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.27","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:13.879883415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","title":"Accessibility pass: focus, alt, heading hierarchy, reduced motion","description":"Full a11y audit over the article once all sections and visualizations are in.

## Checklist
- [ ] Every interactive element has a visible focus ring (Tailwind `focus-visible:ring-*`).
- [ ] Heading hierarchy is correct: one `h1` (hero), then `h2` per section, `h3` per subsection. No skipped levels.
- [ ] Every `<Image>` has meaningful `alt` text; decorative elements use `alt=""`.
- [ ] Every visualization has a descriptive `aria-label` and, where appropriate, a `<figure>` + `<figcaption>` wrapper.
- [ ] All visualizations respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (no animations when set).
- [ ] Keyboard navigation works through every visualization (Tab order makes sense; arrow keys where natural).
- [ ] Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text; ≥ 3:1 for UI state indicators (focus rings, active states).
- [ ] Skip link present (already in site layout, verify not broken).
- [ ] `aria-live` regions for the install sections "copied to clipboard" feedback (if we add that inline here).
- [ ] TOC anchors match section IDs exactly.

## Tools
- `bun lint` (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y should catch the bulk).
- Manual keyboard walk through the whole article.
- Axe DevTools in the browser.

## Acceptance criteria
- Zero a11y warnings from eslint.
- Axe clean on the rendered page.
- Keyboard walk through the article without a mouse: reader can get to every meaningful interaction.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354501572Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:42:49.495786737Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.203071126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.354021373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","title":"Lighthouse / Core Web Vitals check","description":"Run Lighthouse against the article on a dev build + production build.

## Targets
- **LCP** ≤ 2.5s (hero image must use `priority` + blur placeholder; already the pattern).
- **CLS** ≤ 0.1 (visualizations must reserve layout space before mount; use explicit width/height or `aspect-ratio`).
- **INP** ≤ 200ms (scroll-linked effects must be passive; visualizations must not block the main thread on mount).
- **Performance** ≥ 90.
- **Accessibility** = 100.
- **Best Practices** ≥ 95.
- **SEO** = 100.

## If targets fail
- Visualizations too heavy? Check bundle splits: each dynamic import should land in its own chunk. Consider further code-splitting (e.g., antipattern cards flip state machine).
- LCP late? Verify the hero image is in the initial HTML (not SSR-skipped), has `priority`, and uses a proper blur placeholder.
- CLS high? The usual suspect is a visualization whose height isnt reserved; add `min-h-[Npx]` to the viz wrapper.

## Acceptance criteria
- Lighthouse targets met on production build (Vercel preview deploy).
- Any failure is resolved at root cause before merge.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.798249640Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:43:00.532041834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.29","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:16.797700271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-lia","title":"Design Two Worlds visual layout and narrative flow","description":"## Overview
Design the overall layout and narrative structure for the 'Two Worlds' section that bridges Jeffrey's background in large-scale distributed systems with his move into AI/ML.

## Key Elements
- **Visual metaphor**: Two distinct visual 'worlds' that connect/merge
- **Left world**: Enterprise distributed systems (Hadoop, Spark, large clusters)
- **Right world**: AI/ML frontier (LLMs, agents, embeddings)
- **Bridge element**: The connecting narrative showing natural progression

## Content Structure
1. Opening hook: 'From petabyte-scale data to intelligence at scale'
2. Left panel: Enterprise credentials (LinkedIn, Accenture scale)
3. Right panel: AI innovations (current projects)
4. Convergence point: How the skills transfer

## Design Considerations
- Must work on mobile (vertical stack) and desktop (side-by-side)
- Subtle animations on scroll to reveal each 'world'
- Color coding: warm tones for enterprise, cool tones for AI
- Interactive hover states to explore each domain

## Dependencies
This is the foundational design task - all other Two Worlds tasks depend on this.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Figma mockup or detailed wireframe
- [ ] Mobile and desktop layouts defined
- [ ] Animation choreography documented
- [ ] Color palette selected
- [ ] Content outline approved","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:08:25.513812261Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.705724721Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.705724721Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-lkx","title":"Build AI/ML World panel component","description":"## Overview
Create the 'AI/ML World' panel showcasing Jeffrey's current AI work and projects.

## Visual Elements
- Neural network / embedding space visualization
- Project highlights from current portfolio
- Tech stack: Python, PyTorch, LangChain, vector DBs, etc.
- Live project demos or GIFs

## Technical Implementation
- React component with Framer Motion
- Optional: Three.js 3D embedding space visualization
- Integration with existing project data from lib/content.ts
- Performance optimized (lazy load heavy visuals)

## Content
- Hook: 'Building the infrastructure for AI agents'
- Featured projects: Agent Mail, Swiss Army Llama, etc.
- GitHub activity indicator
- 'Currently exploring' section

## Links
- Each project should link to detailed project page
- GitHub links where applicable
- Live demo links

## Dependencies
Depends on design task for layout specs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Component matches design specifications
- [ ] Projects data pulled dynamically from content.ts
- [ ] Smooth animations with reduced motion fallback
- [ ] All links functional and accessible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:08:27.819250291Z","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:30:52.548649008Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:30:52.548554602Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-lkx","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-lia","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-lq3","title":"Update star counts for existing projects in content.ts","description":"## What
Update the badge star counts for existing projects that have grown significantly since last update.

## Why
- Star counts in content.ts badges are outdated
- Accurate star counts demonstrate project traction and credibility
- Some projects have grown 2-5x since last update

## Projects to Update
In jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts, update these projects:

| Project | Old Badge | New Badge |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| mcp_agent_mail | "756 stars" | "1.4K stars" |
| claude_code_agent_farm | "574 stars" | "621 stars" |
| mindmap-generator | "127 stars" | "187 stars" |
| ultimate_bug_scanner | "28 stars" | "132 stars" |
| coding_agent_session_search | (no badge) | "307 stars" |
| ultimate_mcp_client | "139 stars" | "142 stars" |

## Implementation
1. Open jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts
2. Find each project by name in the projects array
3. Update the badge field with new star count
4. For coding_agent_session_search, add badge field if missing

## Notes
- Use "K" notation for counts over 1000 (e.g., "1.4K stars")
- Keep format consistent with existing badges
- Some projects may not have a badge field - add one if appropriate
- llm_aided_ocr is at 2816 stars - could update to "2.8K stars" if currently different

## Acceptance Criteria
- All listed projects have accurate Jan 2026 star counts
- Badge format is consistent across all projects","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:23:03.459751766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:10:43.073524586Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:10:43.073524586Z","close_reason":"Updated star counts for MCP Agent Mail (1.4K), Claude Code Agent Farm (621), Mindmap Generator (187), Ultimate Bug Scanner (132), CASS (307), Ultimate MCP Client (142), and Beads Viewer (891). Also updated flywheelTools star counts.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}
+{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-m5wh","title":"Primer opening callout omits explicit legal disclaimer","description":"Cross-review from hgjp.39: the very top of public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md promises intake and deliverables before it explicitly says the primer is educational only and not legal advice. Tighten the opening callout so the disclaimer is unavoidable on page 1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:54:44.071187574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:55:44.393973980Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:55:44.385145144Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-m5wh","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.39","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T14:54:44.071187574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
